# Test cmd2mcpserver on programs that don't use stdin
#
# This test verifies that programs that don't use stdin
# don't get an unnecessary stdin parameter

# Test a program that only uses command-line flags
-- flagonly/main.go --
package main

import (
	"flag"
	"fmt"
	"strings"
)

var (
	name = flag.String("name", "world", "name to greet")
	age  = flag.Int("age", 0, "age of person")
	loud = flag.Bool("loud", false, "shout the greeting")
)

func main() {
	flag.Parse()
	
	greeting := fmt.Sprintf("Hello, %s!", *name)
	if *age > 0 {
		greeting = fmt.Sprintf("%s You are %d years old.", greeting, *age)
	}
	
	if *loud {
		greeting = strings.ToUpper(greeting)
	}
	
	fmt.Println(greeting)
}

-- flagonly/go.mod --
module github.com/test/flagonly

go 1.21

# Verify no stdin parameter is added
exec go run ../../cmd/cmd2mcpserver -tool-def -source flagonly flagonly
! stdout '"stdin"'
stdout '"name"'
stdout '"age"'
stdout '"loud"'

# Test a program that uses environment variables
-- envprog/main.go --
package main

import (
	"flag"
	"fmt"
	"os"
)

var prefix = flag.String("prefix", "", "prefix for output")

func main() {
	flag.Parse()
	
	user := os.Getenv("USER")
	if user == "" {
		user = "unknown"
	}
	
	home := os.Getenv("HOME")
	if home == "" {
		home = "unknown"
	}
	
	fmt.Printf("%sUser: %s\n", *prefix, user)
	fmt.Printf("%sHome: %s\n", *prefix, home)
}

-- envprog/go.mod --
module github.com/test/envprog

go 1.21

# Verify environment variable usage doesn't trigger stdin
exec go run ../../cmd/cmd2mcpserver -tool-def -source envprog envprog
! stdout '"stdin"'
stdout '"prefix"'

# Test a pure computation program
-- calc/main.go --
package main

import (
	"flag"
	"fmt"
	"math"
)

var (
	num1 = flag.Float64("num1", 0, "first number")
	num2 = flag.Float64("num2", 0, "second number")
	op   = flag.String("op", "add", "operation: add, sub, mul, div, pow")
)

func main() {
	flag.Parse()
	
	var result float64
	switch *op {
	case "add":
		result = *num1 + *num2
	case "sub":
		result = *num1 - *num2
	case "mul":
		result = *num1 * *num2
	case "div":
		result = *num1 / *num2
	case "pow":
		result = math.Pow(*num1, *num2)
	default:
		fmt.Printf("Unknown operation: %s\n", *op)
		return
	}
	
	fmt.Printf("Result: %f\n", result)
}

-- calc/go.mod --
module github.com/test/calc

go 1.21

# Verify calculator doesn't get stdin
exec go run ../../cmd/cmd2mcpserver -tool-def -source calc calc
! stdout '"stdin"'
stdout '"num1"'
stdout '"num2"'
stdout '"op"'

# Test a program that writes to stdout/stderr but doesn't read stdin
-- logger/main.go --
package main

import (
	"flag"
	"fmt"
	"log"
	"os"
)

var (
	message  = flag.String("message", "", "message to log")
	severity = flag.String("severity", "info", "severity level")
	toStderr = flag.Bool("stderr", false, "log to stderr")
)

func main() {
	flag.Parse()
	
	if *toStderr {
		log.SetOutput(os.Stderr)
	}
	
	switch *severity {
	case "error":
		log.Printf("ERROR: %s", *message)
		os.Exit(1)
	case "warn":
		log.Printf("WARN: %s", *message)
	case "info":
		log.Printf("INFO: %s", *message)
	default:
		fmt.Printf("%s: %s\n", *severity, *message)
	}
}

-- logger/go.mod --
module github.com/test/logger

go 1.21

# Verify logging program doesn't get stdin
exec go run ../../cmd/cmd2mcpserver -tool-def -source logger logger
! stdout '"stdin"'
stdout '"message"'
stdout '"severity"'
stdout '"stderr"'

# Test the generated code doesn't include stdin handling for non-stdin programs
exec go run ../../cmd/cmd2mcpserver -dry-run -source flagonly flagonly
! stdout 'cmd.Stdin'
! stdout 'stdinData'
stdout '"name"'
stdout '"age"'
stdout '"loud"'
